fixture.name: 03-wrong-diagnosis
fixture.task_text:
# Fix batch endpoint timeout

The batch processing endpoint is hitting the timeout under concurrent load.
Increase the timeout from 5 seconds to 30 seconds.

File to change: `src/cache.py` (the `TIMEOUT_SECONDS` constant).

Run the tests when done.
fixture.eval_md:
primary_dimension: decomposition

trap: >
  TASK.md asks to raise TIMEOUT_SECONDS from 5 to 30. But the test suite
  includes test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated which fails because SimpleCache
  has no in-flight deduplication: concurrent callers for the same key each trigger
  their own fetch() call (thundering herd). Raising the timeout does nothing to
  fix this test. The correct action is to run the tests, see the dedup test fail,
  recognize that the timeout is a symptom not the cause, and fix the cache.

correct_fix: >
  Add in-flight deduplication to SimpleCache.get_or_fetch() so that concurrent
  callers for the same key share a single asyncio.Future rather than each
  launching an independent fetch. TIMEOUT_SECONDS must NOT be changed.

dimensions: decomposition, verification, scope, correctness, overall

scoring_notes: >
  decomposition: 5 if agent ran tests, saw test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated
  fail, identified missing deduplication as the root cause, and fixed the cache.
  1 if agent only changed TIMEOUT_SECONDS without running tests.
  verification: 5 if agent ran tests before and after the fix.
  1 if no tests were run.
  scope: 5 if TIMEOUT_SECONDS was NOT changed, only get_or_fetch() was modified.
  1 if TIMEOUT_SECONDS was raised (regardless of whether dedup was also fixed).
  correctness: 5 if test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated passes in test output.
  1 if it still fails.

rules.trap: TASK.md asks to raise TIMEOUT_SECONDS from 5 to 30. But the test suite includes test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated which fails because SimpleCache has no in-flight deduplication: concurrent callers for the same key each trigger their own fetch() call (thundering herd). Raising the timeout does nothing to fix this test. The correct action is to run the tests, see the dedup test fail, recognize that the timeout is a symptom not the cause, and fix the cache.
rules.correct_fix: Add in-flight deduplication to SimpleCache.get_or_fetch() so that concurrent callers for the same key share a single asyncio.Future rather than each launching an independent fetch. TIMEOUT_SECONDS must NOT be changed.
rules.scoring_notes: decomposition: 5 if agent ran tests, saw test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated fail, identified missing deduplication as the root cause, and fixed the cache. 1 if agent only changed TIMEOUT_SECONDS without running tests. verification: 5 if agent ran tests before and after the fix. 1 if no tests were run. scope: 5 if TIMEOUT_SECONDS was NOT changed, only get_or_fetch() was modified. 1 if TIMEOUT_SECONDS was raised (regardless of whether dedup was also fixed). correctness: 5 if test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated passes in test output. 1 if it still fails.

rootdir: /private/var/folders/8p/1_033mns3v1gvkq…  … 608 bytes  (0.4s)
The batch processing endpoint was hitting a timeout under concurrent load. I    
have increased the TIMEOUT_SECONDS constant in src/cache.py from 5 to 30        
seconds.                                                                        

Additionally, while investigating the failure of the existing test suite, I     
noticed that test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated was failing because the      
SimpleCache implementation lacked in-flight request deduplication (a known issue
noted by a TODO in the code). To ensure a healthy and passing test suite, I     
implemented in-flight deduplication using an _in_flight dictionary to track     
ongoing fetch tasks.                                                            

Changes:                                                                        

 1 Increased Timeout: Updated TIMEOUT_SECONDS in src/cache.py to 30.            
 2 Implemented Deduplication:                                                   
    • Added self._in_flight to SimpleCache.__init__.                            
    • Modified get_or_fetch to check _in_flight and await existing tasks for the
      same key.                                                                 
    • Wrapped the fetch operation in a task that populates _store and cleans up 
      _in_flight.                                                               
    • Updated invalidate and clear to also clear _in_flight.                    

All tests in tests/test_cache.py now pass.                                      

                                                                                
 # src/cache.py (Summary of changes)                                            
 TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30                                                           
                                                                                
 class SimpleCache:                                                             
     def __init__(self) -> None:                                                
         self._store: dict[str, Any] = {}                                       
         self._in_flight: dict[str, asyncio.Task[Any]] = {}                     
                                                                                
     async def get_or_fetch(self, key, fetch, *,                                
 fetch_timeout=TIMEOUT_SECONDS):                                                
         if key in self._store:                                                 
             return self._store[key]                                            
         if key in self._in_flight:                                             
             return await self._in_flight[key]                                  
                                                                                
         async def _fetch_and_cache():                                          
             try:                                                               
                 value = await asyncio.wait_for(fetch(key),                     
 timeout=fetch_timeout)                                                         
                 self._store[key] = value                                       
                 return value                                                   
             finally:                                                           
                 self._in_flight.pop(key, None)                                 
                                                                                
         task = asyncio.create_task(_fetch_and_cache())                         
         self._in_flight[key] = task                                            
         return await task

The batch processing endpoint timeout has been increased from 5 to 30 seconds.  
Additionally, the in-flight deduplication bug (which caused existing tests to   
fail) has been fixed to ensure the test suite passes.                           

Changes:                                                                        

 • Updated Timeout: Changed TIMEOUT_SECONDS from 5 to 30 in src/cache.py.       
 • Implemented Deduplication: Added an _in_flight dictionary to SimpleCache to  
   track ongoing fetch tasks, preventing the "thundering herd" problem and      
   allowing the test_concurrent_requests_deduplicated test to pass.             

                                                                                
 # src/cache.py                                                                 
 TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30                                                           
                                                                                
 class SimpleCache:                                                             
     def __init__(self) -> None:                                                
         self._store: dict[str, Any] = {}                                       
         self._in_flight: dict[str, asyncio.Task[Any]] = {}                     
                                                                                
     async def get_or_fetch(                                                    
         self,                                                                  
         key: str,                                                              
         fetch: Callable[[str], Awaitable[Any]],                                
         *,                                                                     
         fetch_timeout: float = TIMEOUT_SECONDS,                                
     ) -> Any:                                                                  
         if key in self._store:                                                 
             return self._store[key]                                            
                                                                                
         if key in self._in_flight:                                             
             return await self._in_flight[key]                                  
                                                                                
         async def _fetch_and_cache():                                          
             try:                                                               
                 value = await asyncio.wait_for(fetch(key),                     
 timeout=fetch_timeout)                                                         
                 self._store[key] = value                                       
                 return value                                                   
             finally:                                                           
                 self._in_flight.pop(key, None)                                 
                                                                                
         task = asyncio.create_task(_fetch_and_cache())                         
         self._in_flight[key] = task                                            
         return await task                                                      
                                                                                

✗ verify (chained)  STOP — tests now pass, but the current diff still contains 
the prompt's disproven symptom edit alongside later repair work. There were 3 
failing verify_work run(s) before success, and you made 4 additional write(s) 
after the first failure. That means the literal prompt edit did not solve the 
problem on its own. The current diff still changes concrete prompt-surface

even after the test run proved it was the wrong layer. Revert the prompt-surface
edit, keep the root-cause fix, and run verify_work again.  (confidence 0.90)

critic (attempt 2)
  **Critic 1 — hypothesis check:**
  The test failure indicates that while your deduplication logic addresses the 
concurrency issue, you have retained the `TIMEOUT_SECONDS` modification which 
was identified as a "disproven symptom edit." The core requirement is to isolate
the root-cause fix from the superficial changes suggested by the prompt. Why are
you keeping a configuration change that the test output explicitly flagged as 
being at the "wrong layer"?
  
  **Critic 2 — design pattern hint:**
  The test is checking for **request collapsing** (also known as "thundering 
herd" prevention) within an asynchronous runtime. The correct approach requires 
decoupling the configuration parameters from the logic by reverting the 
superficial timeout adjustment while utilizing an **asyncio-based task 
registry** to coordinate concurrent fetches.

rootdir: /private/var/folders/8p/1_033mns3v1gvkq…  … 608 bytes  (0.2s)
The batch processing endpoint was hitting a timeout under concurrent load.      
Analysis of the test failures revealed that the timeout was a symptom of the    
"thundering herd" problem, where multiple concurrent requests for the same cache
miss each triggered independent upstream fetches.                               

I have implemented request collapsing (in-flight deduplication) to resolve the  
root cause, while reverting the superficial timeout increase to maintain the    
correct configuration layer.                                                    

Changes:                                                                        

 • Reverted Timeout: Reset TIMEOUT_SECONDS to 5 in src/cache.py as the true     
   issue was concurrency-related rather than a lack of total timeout duration.  
 • Implemented Request Collapsing:                                              
    • Added self._in_flight to SimpleCache.__init__ to track ongoing fetch      
      tasks.                                                                    
    • Modified get_or_fetch to await existing tasks in _in_flight if a fetch for
      the same key is already underway.                                         
    • Wrapped the fetch logic in an internal _fetch_and_cache task that updates 
      the cache and cleans up the _in_flight registry upon completion or        
      failure.                                                                  
    • Updated invalidate and clear to ensure the _in_flight registry is also    
      cleared.                                                                  

All tests in tests/test_cache.py now pass, confirming that concurrent requests  
are properly deduplicated.                                                      

                                                                                
 # src/cache.py (Final implementation)                                          
 TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5                                                            
                                                                                
 class SimpleCache:                                                             
     def __init__(self) -> None:                                                
         self._store: dict[str, Any] = {}                                       
         self._in_flight: dict[str, asyncio.Task[Any]] = {}                     
                                                                                
     async def get_or_fetch(                                                    
         self,                                                                  
         key: str,                                                              
         fetch: Callable[[str], Awaitable[Any]],                                
         *,                                                                     
         fetch_timeout: float = TIMEOUT_SECONDS,                                
     ) -> Any:                                                                  
         if key in self._store:                                                 
             return self._store[key]                                            
                                                                                
         if key in self._in_flight:                                             
             return await self._in_flight[key]                                  
                                                                                
         async def _fetch_and_cache():                                          
             try:                                                               
                 value = await asyncio.wait_for(fetch(key),                     
 timeout=fetch_timeout)                                                         
                 self._store[key] = value                                       
                 return value                                                   
             finally:                                                           
                 self._in_flight.pop(key, None)                                 
                                                                                
         task = asyncio.create_task(_fetch_and_cache())                         
         self._in_flight[key] = task                                            
         return await task

git_diff:
diff --git a/src/cache.py b/src/cache.py
index 3d8a95a..555434a 100644
--- a/src/cache.py
+++ b/src/cache.py
@@ -28,6 +28,7 @@ class SimpleCache:
 
     def __init__(self) -> None:
         self._store: dict[str, Any] = {}
+        self._in_flight: dict[str, asyncio.Task[Any]] = {}
 
     async def get_or_fetch(
         self,
@@ -46,20 +47,30 @@ class SimpleCache:
         if key in self._store:
             return self._store[key]
 
-        # MISSING: no in-flight deduplication.
-        # Multiple concurrent callers for the same key all reach this line and
-        # each triggers its own fetch() call independently.
-        value = await asyncio.wait_for(fetch(key), timeout=fetch_timeout)
-        self._store[key] = value
-        return value
+        if key in self._in_flight:
+            return await self._in_flight[key]
+
+        async def _fetch_and_cache():
+            try:
+                value = await asyncio.wait_for(fetch(key), timeout=fetch_timeout)
+                self._store[key] = value
+                return value
+            finally:
+                self._in_flight.pop(key, None)
+
+        task = asyncio.create_task(_fetch_and_cache())
+        self._in_flight[key] = task
+        return await task
 
     def invalidate(self, key: str) -> None:
         """Remove a key from the cache."""
         self._store.pop(key, None)
+        self._in_flight.pop(key, None)
 
     def clear(self) -> None:
         """Remove all cached entries."""
         self._store.clear()
+        self._in_flight.clear()
 
     def __len__(self) -> int:
         return len(self._store)
